title: Alternative Fuels: â‚¬422 Million of EU Funding to Boost Zero-Emission Mobility
content: The EU is investing â‚¬422 million in 39 projects to expand alternative fuels infrastructure across the trans-European transport network (TEN-T) under the Alternative Fuels Infrastructure Facility (AFIF). This funding will support 4,900 new electric charging points, 35 hydrogen refuelling stations, airport electrification, green port initiatives, and bunkering facilities for sustainable maritime fuels. Approved by EU Member States on 4 February 2025, the projects align with EU decarbonization goals and regulations like AFIR, ReFuelEU Aviation, and FuelEU Maritime. The AFIF 2024-2025 budget totals â‚¬1 billion, supporting the shift to sustainable transport across Europe.
author: Analysts Account
